title: Lines, Containers, Headings, Paragraphs, Lists, Quotes, and Fenced Code
description: Blank lines erase structure, list markers nest by guesswork, headings skip source evidence, a fence closes with the wrong marker, and paragraphs swallow following blocks. Learners cumulatively produce line-oriented block parser with explicit continuation rules through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Represent line content, indentation, blankness, offset, tab expansion, newline, and container prefix without discarding source positions.
Apply named block-start and continuation precedence so paragraphs, headings, quotes, lists, thematic breaks, and code resolve consistently.
Parse ATX headings and paragraphs with closing-marker, interruption, inline-content, and empty-heading evidence.
